actionLibraryList.title: Aerobic Skiing (Straight Arms)
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand on the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart, knees and hips slightly bent, and lean slightly forward while gripping the handles.
2.Engage your core and back, pulling the handles down until your arms swing back past your thighs, keeping the arms straight.
3.Bend and extend the knees in a fluid motion, driving the handles downward by hinging at the hips. Exhale while pulling, inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the grips to the high position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Position your feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips with a slight bend in the knees, and grip the handles with your hands
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: The knees bend and extend in a smooth motion, while the hips push back and descend.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your arms straight, swing them up and down, and extend your arms alongside your hips.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: As you pull the handles down towards your hips, engage the muscles of your back and lower body.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Maintain an upright posture with your back straight.\n2.Keep a slight bend in the knees and hips.\n3.Slightly lean forward while gripping the handles.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750673145702.jpg"},{"context":"1.Engage your core and back muscles, pulling the handles down until your arms swing past your thighs. Keep your arms straight throughout the movement.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750673149182.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Engage your core throughout the movement. Exhale as you pull, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: As you pull the handles down towards your hips, engage the muscles of your back and lower body.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your arms straight, swing them up and down, and extend your arms alongside your hips.

actionLibraryList.title: Bodyweight Alternating Forward Lunges
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Start standing with your core engaged. Step one leg forward into a lunge, lowering your body until the front knee is aligned with your toes and the back knee is close to the ground without touching it. 
2.Exhale as you push back to standing through the front foot, then switch legs and repeat. Keep your torso upright and your hips facing forward. 
3.Avoid letting your front knee cave inward or extend beyond your toes.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749786633555.jpg","context":"1.Align the front knee with the toes of the front foot.\n2.Lower the rear knee toward the ground without letting it touch the floor."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749786679773.jpg","context":"1.Push through the front heel to return to standing, then switch legs and repeat."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you stand up, inhale as you lower down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: You should feel strong activation in your legs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid letting your knees cave inward or extend past your toes.

actionLibraryList.title: Alternating Reverse Lunge
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Keep your chest up, back straight, and core engaged.
2.Keep both knees pointing forward.
3.Step one leg back and lower until both knees are at 90°.
4.Return to standing, then alternate to the other leg.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Step one leg back into a single-leg squat.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Keep both knees bent at ninety degrees.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep both knees pointing straight forward.\n2.Step one leg back and lower until both knees are at 90°.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749800497307.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid letting your knees go past your toes.

actionLibraryList.title: Dynamic Shoulder Circles
actionLibraryList.context: Lift your head, straighten your chest, and perform shoulder circles to the fullest range.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Touch your fingers to your shoulders and draw circles using your shoulders as the pivot point.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749524137343.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: This movement can enhance the mobility of your shoulder joint.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your body still and avoid swaying.

actionLibraryList.title: Rotator Cuff Stimulation
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand and keep your upper arms parallel to the ground as you open up.                                                                                 2.The elbow joint is the fixed point for external rotation; keep your elbow at a 90-degree angle throughout the movement.                                    
3.Keep breathing and activate your rotator cuff.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Stand in a neutral position.\n2.Keep your upper arms parallel to the ground as you open up.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749717912465.jpg"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749717915966.jpg","context":"1.Using the elbow as the pivot point, perform an external rotation while maintaining a 90-degree angle at the elbow joint."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: This movement can activate your shoulder rotator cuff muscles.

actionLibraryList.title: Dynamic Hamstring Stretch
actionLibraryList.context: Keep your upper body upright and bend forward as far as you can.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1753410285189.jpg","context":"1.Front leg straight, dorsiflex the foot.\n2.Back leg slightly bent.\n3.Shift your weight onto the back leg."}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Feel the stretch in the back of your thighs.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid rounding your shoulders or arching your back during the movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Incline Barbell Bench Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Set the bench to a 30–45° incline and lie back.
2.Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width.
3.Exhale, press the bar above the clavicles, elbows nearly straight but not locked.
4.Inhale, slowly lower the bar to the upper chest midline, keeping elbows pointing down.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the incline bench to a 30-degree angle
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Lie on the bench and grip the barbell with both hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Chest up, and engage your core
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Your forearms should be perpendicular to the floor.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you pull the barbell upward, and inhale as you lower the barbell back down
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Push the barbell to the clavicle level, engaging your pectorals.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Lie back on the incline bench with your back fully supported against the pad.\n2.Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748936873732.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep elbows nearly straight without locking them.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748936878894.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale to press up, inhale to lower down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Lower to stretch the upper chest, press up to contract and squeeze the chest.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep elbows from flaring out to avoid excessive shoulder stress.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Bent Over Row
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent, and core engaged.
2.Hinge forward 45°–60°, keeping a neutral spine.
3.Grip the barbell overhand, hands shoulder-width or slightly wider.
4.Keep elbows close to your sides and engage your lats.
5.Pull the barbell up along your front to lower chest or abdomen.
6.Pause briefly at the top, then return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Set the machine attachment mounts to the floor position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Lean forward by hinging at the hips while keeping your back straight and your knees slightly bent.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Keep your core engaged to stabilize yourself.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your elbows tucked and engage your core, glutes, and quads for stability.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Keep your breathing steady throughout. Exhale as you pull the barbell up, inhale as you lower it back slowly.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your back muscles to drive your arms as you pull the barbell up. Hold for a few seconds, feeling your back muscles contract.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Depress your shoulders.\n2.Hinge forward 45°–60°.\n3.Grip the barbell overhand.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749001869394.jpg"},{"context":"1.Keep your elbows close to your sides.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749001873009.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while pulling up, inhale while lowering.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your upper body stable, avoiding forward or backward sway and using momentum.

actionLibraryList.title: Barbell Push Press
actionLibraryList.context: Stand shoulder-width apart with the barbell in the front rack position and core engaged. Squat slightly to generate power, then explosively push through your legs and extend your hips to drive the barbell overhead. Pause at the top, then control the descent back to the starting position. Focus on using your legs for power, with shoulders and arms finishing the press.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750670505392.jpg","context":"1.Place the barbell on the front rack position with both hands gripping the bar, keeping your forearms vertical to the ground.\n2.Bend your knees and hips slightly to generate power for the movement."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750670509596.jpg","context":"1.Keep your arms directly above your shoulders.\n2.Extend your elbows fully, but don't lock them."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you press the barbell up, inhale as you lower it back down.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Explode through your legs to transfer power to your upper body, with your shoulders and arms completing the final press.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid pushing only with the arms, failing to drive through the legs, and letting the bar deviate from the midline.

actionLibraryList.title: Standing Tricep Rope High-to-Low Woodchops
actionLibraryList.context: Stand in the center of the cable machine, holding the dual ropes with both hands. Extend your arms above your head slightly in front, with palms facing each other. Position your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width, in a staggered or lunge stance. Engage your core, maintain a neutral spine, and depress your scapula. Initiate the movement from your core, as both hands together bring the handle along a diagonal path downward to one side, while rotating your upper body. Feel the contraction of your obliques and rectus abdominis working together.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751510043560.jpg","context":"1.Hold the dual ropes with both hands, extending your arms above your head slightly in front, with palms facing each other."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1751510048298.jpg","context":"1.Using both hands together, chop the handle downward to one side along a diagonal path."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you swing downward, inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your core to drive the movement.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Avoid shrugging your shoulders, arching your lower back, or swinging for momentum. Keep your pelvis stable throughout the movement.

actionLibraryList.title: Burpees
actionLibraryList.context: Quickly switch between each phase while maintaining body stability, and keep your core engaged throughout to protect your lower back.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Perform a push-up, then explosively jump up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Drop your legs into a push-up position. Perform a push-up, then return your legs and step up, extending your arms overhead as you stand up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Initiate the jump-into-the-air movement by engaging your abdominal muscles.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Brace your core and engage your abdominal muscles.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Lower your chest until it nearly touches the ground.
2.Keep your elbows flared out to the sides.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750041773473.jpg"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750041776935.jpg","context":"1.Slightly bend your knees to absorb the impact upon landing.
2.Keep your core tight throughout the jump.
"}]
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Brace your core and engage your abdominal muscles.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Initiate the jump-into-the-air movement by engaging your abdominal muscles.

actionLibraryList.title: Aerobic Rowing
actionLibraryList.context:
1.A rowing bench and a barbell bar are required for this exercise.
2.Sit on the bench with the balls of your feet securely placed in the straps and hold the barbell with an overhand grip (palms facing down). Keep your upper body upright.
3.Engage your back and drive the barbell toward your abdomen by extending through your hips and legs. Keep your elbows slightly bent and lean back slightly.
4.Maintain an upright posture, avoiding excessive leaning back. Exhale as you pull the barbell, and inhale as you return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: A rowing bench and a barbell bar are required for this exercise.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Grip the short barbell with both hands in an overhand grip, place your feet securely on the foot pedals, and fasten the straps.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Keep your elbows close to your torso.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Keep your upper body upright, avoid leaning too far backward.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale while pulling, inhale as you return to the catch position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your back and glutes/legs simultaneously, then pull the cable backward.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Grip the barbell with palms facing down.
2.Sit on the bench.
3.Keep your back engaged and drive the barbell towards your abdomen using your hips and legs.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750673090988.jpg"},{"context":"1.Arm extended backward.
2.Elbow joint flexed.
3.Bodyweight tilts backward.
4.Feet positioned in a front-to-back stance, extending the posterior leg muscles.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750673095049.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while pulling, inhale as you return to the catch position.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage your back and glutes/legs simultaneously, then pull the cable backward.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your upper body upright, avoid leaning too far backward.
